meaning: adj.不容争辩的,没有疑问的
examples: The point was inarguable; the cop walked away.
1979 was the year they burst into inarguable reality.
With the inarguable proofs, he had to admitted his guilt.
"The word, absolutely, is like saying that this is positively true -- this is inarguable, " Thompson says.
And I think it is inarguable that we have made progress in that effort, but that effort continues.
